primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable can be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 hedge accounting requirements

primary_authority.reason: Section 6.3.5 establishes the fundamental requirement that hedged items must be with external parties; Section 6.3.6 provides the specific exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items qualifying as hedged items in consolidated financial statements when the risk affects consolidated profit or loss

supporting_authority.reason: Paragraph 32 addresses exchange differences on monetary items forming part of net investment in foreign operations; Paragraph 45 confirms intragroup monetary items cannot be eliminated on consolidation and create FX exposure

Provides guidance on hedge accounting for net investment in foreign operations, clarifying eligible hedged risks and the requirement that hedge accounting applies only to FX differences between functional currencies of parent and foreign operation

peripheral_authority.reason: These sections address general recognition of FX gains/losses, FVOCI measurement, and group hedging rules but do not specifically address the eligibility of intragroup items as hedged items
Cash flow presentation requirements for interest and dividends are not relevant to determining hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup FX exposures
Related party disclosure requirements are topically related but do not govern hedge accounting treatment for intragroup transactions
Addresses non-reciprocal distributions of assets as dividends but does not address hedge accounting for FX risk on intragroup dividend receivables
Addresses changes in tax status and is not relevant to hedge accounting for foreign currency risk

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements
Hedge of net investment in foreign operation

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 explicitly permits foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the FX differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and will affect consolidated profit or loss, creating a specific alternative model distinct from the general external-party requirement in paragraph 6.3.5

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 clarifies that hedge accounting may be applied to foreign currency risk arising from net investment in foreign operations; if the dividend receivable relates to a foreign subsidiary and meets net investment hedge criteria, this represents a distinct top-level approach under IFRS 9 hedge accounting framework
